name: rspec
description: RSpec is a Behaviour-Driven Development (BDD) testing framework for Ruby that provides expressive syntax, powerful matchers, mocking/stubbing, and detailed output for writing and running automated tests.

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a project for RSpec by creating .rspec config and spec/spec_helper.rb files

subcommands.name: spec/
subcommands.description: Run all spec files in the spec/ directory

args.name: files_or_dirs
args.description: Specific spec files, directories, or line references to run

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: progress (default), documentation, html, json

options.name: --color
options.description: Enable color output

options.name: --no-color
options.description: Disable color output

options.name: --profile
options.description: Show the N slowest examples (default: 10)

options.name: --fail-fast
options.description: Stop running after the first failure

options.name: --order
options.description: Run examples in specified order: defined, random, rand:SEED

options.name: --seed
options.description: Equivalent to --order rand:SEED for reproducible random order

options.name: --tag
options.description: Run only examples with the specified tag

options.name: --exclude-tag
options.description: Exclude examples with the specified tag

options.name: --example
options.description: Run examples whose full description matches the given string

options.name: --pattern
options.description: Load files matching the given pattern (default: spec/**/*_spec.rb)

options.name: --exclude-pattern
options.description: Exclude files matching the pattern from loading

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Print the formatter output without running any examples

options.name: --warnings
options.description: Enable Ruby warnings

options.name: --require
options.description: Require a Ruby file before loading spec files

options.name: --only-failures
options.description: Run only previously failed examples

options.name: --next-failure
options.description: Run only the next failure (equivalent to --only-failures --fail-fast)

options.name: --backtrace
options.description: Enable full backtrace instead of filtered

options.name: --out
options.description: Write output to a file instead of STDOUT

options.name: --bisect
options.description: Find the minimal set of examples that reproduce a failure

options.name: --force-color
options.description: Force color output even when not connected to a TTY

subcommands.name: run single file
subcommands.description: Execute specs from a single file, optionally at a specific line

args.name: file
args.description: Path to the spec file, optionally with :LINE_NUMBER

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format

options.name: --fail-fast
options.description: Abort after first failure

options.name: --backtrace
options.description: Show full backtrace

subcommands.name: run by tag
subcommands.description: Run only examples annotated with specific tags or metadata

options.name: --tag
options.description: Include examples with this tag

options.name: --exclude-tag
options.description: Exclude examples with this tag

subcommands.name: run by example name
subcommands.description: Run examples whose full description matches a string or pattern

options.name: --example
options.description: Run examples matching this string/regex

subcommands.name: bisect
subcommands.description: Use bisection to find the minimal set of examples that reproduce an ordering-dependent failure

options.name: --seed
options.description: Seed for the random order that produced the failure

options.name: --bisect
options.description: Enable bisect mode

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format

subcommands.name: format output
subcommands.description: Run specs with different output formatters for CI, documentation, or debugging

options.name: --format
options.description: Formatter: progress, documentation, html, json, failures

options.name: --out
options.description: Output file for the formatter

subcommands.name: only-failures
subcommands.description: Re-run only the examples that failed in the previous run

options.name: --only-failures
options.description: Run only previously failed examples

options.name: --next-failure
options.description: Run only the next failure, then stop

subcommands.name: profile
subcommands.description: Display the slowest examples to identify performance bottlenecks

options.name: --profile
options.description: Show N slowest examples

subcommands.name: random order
subcommands.description: Run examples in random order to detect order-dependent failures

options.name: --order
options.description: Ordering method: defined, random, rand:SEED, recently-modified

options.name: --seed
options.description: Set the random seed for reproducible ordering

subcommands.name: parallel specs
subcommands.description: Run specs in parallel using parallel_tests gem integration (when configured)

options.name: --pattern
options.description: File pattern to match

subcommands.name: dry-run
subcommands.description: Print formatted output without actually running any examples

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Do not run examples, just print them

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format

globalOptions.name: --format
globalOptions.description: Choose output format: progress (default), documentation, html, json

globalOptions.name: --color
globalOptions.description: Enable color in the output

globalOptions.name: --no-color
globalOptions.description: Disable color in the output

globalOptions.name: --force-color
globalOptions.description: Force color output even without a TTY

globalOptions.name: --profile
globalOptions.description: Show the N slowest examples and example groups

globalOptions.name: --no-profile
globalOptions.description: Disable profiling of slow examples

globalOptions.name: --fail-fast
globalOptions.description: Abort the run after the first failure

globalOptions.name: --failure-exit-code
globalOptions.description: Override the exit code used when there are failing specs

globalOptions.name: --order
globalOptions.description: Run examples in the specified order: defined, random, rand:SEED, recently-modified

globalOptions.name: --seed
globalOptions.description: Random seed for ordering (equivalent to --order rand:SEED)

globalOptions.name: --bisect
globalOptions.description: Find the minimal reproduction set for ordering-dependent failures

globalOptions.name: --tag
globalOptions.description: Run only examples with the given tag (use ~tag to exclude)

globalOptions.name: --exclude-tag
globalOptions.description: Exclude examples with the given tag

globalOptions.name: --example
globalOptions.description: Run examples whose full nested description matches this string

globalOptions.name: --pattern
globalOptions.description: Glob pattern to load spec files (default: spec/**/*_spec.rb)

globalOptions.name: --exclude-pattern
globalOptions.description: Glob pattern to exclude from loading

globalOptions.name: --dry-run
globalOptions.description: Print formatter output without running examples

globalOptions.name: --warnings
globalOptions.description: Enable Ruby warnings

globalOptions.name: --require
globalOptions.description: Require a Ruby file before loading specs

globalOptions.name: --init
globalOptions.description: Initialize a project with .rspec and spec/spec_helper.rb

globalOptions.name: --only-failures
globalOptions.description: Filter to run only examples that failed on the previous run

globalOptions.name: --next-failure
globalOptions.description: Apply --only-failures and --fail-fast together

globalOptions.name: --backtrace
globalOptions.description: Enable full backtrace output

globalOptions.name: --out
globalOptions.description: Write output to a file instead of $stdout

globalOptions.name: --deprecation-out
globalOptions.description: Write deprecation warnings to a file

globalOptions.name: --default-path
globalOptions.description: Set the default path where RSpec looks for spec files

globalOptions.name: --error-exit-code
globalOptions.description: Exit code to use when there is an error loading specs

globalOptions.name: --version
globalOptions.description: Display the installed RSpec version

globalOptions.name: --help
globalOptions.description: Display help information

detectors.name: rspec_config_exists
detectors.description: Detect .rspec configuration file in the current directory

detectors.name: spec_dir_exists
detectors.description: Detect spec/ directory indicating RSpec is in use

detectors.name: spec_helper_exists
detectors.command: test -f spec/spec_helper.rb && echo 'spec_helper' || (test -f spec/rails_helper.rb && echo 'rails_helper' || echo 'not_found')

detectors.name: rspec_in_gemfile
detectors.description: Check if rspec is listed in the Gemfile

detectors.name: spec_file_count
detectors.description: Count the number of spec files
